summary: Preoperative tracheobronchoscopy helps confirm airway patency and anatomical integrity in newborns with esophageal atresia, which is critical due to potential associated upper airway abnormalities. This assessment supports safe surgical planning by identifying any obstructive or structural issues affecting ventilation before corrective procedures are performed.
